intro: The clearest split is that 1Password carries medium lock-in and CyberArk Conjur carries high lock-in. 1Password is Secrets Management tool for developers; CyberArk Conjur is Enterprise secrets management platform with dynamic secrets, RBAC, and audit trails for DevOps pipelines. The data points 1Password at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars, and CyberArk Conjur at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars. Pricing: 1Password is subscription from $2.99; CyberArk Conjur is subscription from 60. Lock-in is medium for 1Password and high for CyberArk Conjur, with DX at 4/5 vs 3/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 2/5. On listed strengths, 1Password emphasises teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars, and CyberArk Conjur emphasises teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits solo, static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: 1Password is for teams; CyberArk Conjur is for teams; decide based on how much lock-in you tolerate.
verdict_a: Choose 1Password if you can accept medium lock-in in exchange for scope and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.
verdict_b: Choose CyberArk Conjur if deeper lock-in is acceptable for the feature depth and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $2.99 for 1Password and 60 for CyberArk Conjur. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from 1Password to CyberArk Conjur?
faq.a: 1Password is tagged with medium lock-in (items exportable to 1pux/json but deeply integrated workflows). CyberArk Conjur is tagged with high lock-in (enterprise secrets lock-in). At least one side is high lock-in, so budget migration time for re-implementing integrations.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, 1Password or CyberArk Conjur?
faq.a: The dataset rates 1Password at 4/5 for developer experience and CyberArk Conjur at 3/5, so 1Password scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is CyberArk Conjur a good alternative to 1Password?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches teams, since both list the same fit. CyberArk Conjur is flagged as not for solo, static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
